steps: Setting up golden gate reactions: We recommend setting up 7 sample reactions and 1 negative control with no insert. A B C D 1 Total samples # 7 2 Component Final conc. 1Rxn (uL) Total 3 H 2 O Up to 25 uL 4 T4 DNA ligase buffer 1X 2.5 18.4 5 T4 DNA ligase (2,000U/ul) 0.5 3.7 6 Plasmid (100ng/ul) 250 ng 2.5 9.849 7 Esp3I (10U/ul) 30U 2 14.7 8 Sub-total 21.2 155.82 9 Barcode amplicon Insert (ul) 1:3 mol ratio Variable Variable 10 Total 25 #REF! Aliquot 25 µL per reaction to 8-well strip tube. Set the thermal cycler for the program below: A B C 1 Step Temperature Time (hh:mm:ss) 2 1 37°C 00:05:00 3 2 16°C 00:05:00 4 3 Go to step 1 for 20-30 total cycles 5 4 60°C 00:15:00 6 5 85°C 00:15:00 7 6 4°C hold

Make digestion mix: A B C D 1 Total samples # 8 2 Component Final conc. 1Rxn (uL) Total 3 H 2 O 3 25.2 4 rCutSmart Buffer 1 8.4 5 BsmBI-v2 (10U/ul) 10U 1 8.4 6 Total 5 #REF! Aliquot 5 µL to each golden gate reaction. Set the thermal cycler for the program below: A B C 1 Step Temperature Time (hh:mm:ss) 2 1 55°C At least 2 hours 3 2 80°C 00:15:00 4 3 Hold at 4°C
Purify with Zymo Clean & Concentrator -5 kit: pooling 3-4 reactions for each column with final elute to 10 µL .
Measure the final elute with Nanodrop to look for normal purify curve, and quantify with Qubit: final reaction elution should be > 5ng/ul. The negative control should be < 5ng/ul.

Depending on the final Golden gate concentration and volume, aim for˜ 15 ng - 20 ng of DNA per 25 µL of NEB-beta for each sample reaction. Keep total volume of input DNA < 3 µL to avoid cell death. Use 1 µL for controls: golden gate negative control, Puc 19 control, and H 2 O control.

For each vector to be barcoded, aim for at least 4x 10 8 total barcodes. This requires plating 2-3 of 245 mm plates. Each 245 mm plate can hold no more than 4 electro-competent reactions recovered in no more than 4 mL of SOC.
Pre-chill cuvettes On ice for at least 00:15:00 .
Aliquot 875 µL of SOC media for each electroporation reaction to glass tubes. Pre-warm the tubes with media and an additional bottle of SOC in 37 °C .
Thaw vials NEB electrocompetent cells (100ul/tube) On ice -immediately before the transformation. The cells start to die after ~ 00:05:00 .

Add 25 µL thawed cells to each cuvette by tilting each cuvette towards you for easier pipetting. Stop at the 1st pipette stop to avoid injecting air bubbles to the cuvette. Air will cause your samples to “arc”.
Add DNA to each sample to its cuvette into the cells with the cuvette tilted. Again, stop at the 1st pipette stop to avoid injecting air bubbles to the cuvette. Air will cause your samples to “arc”.
Flick and tap cuvette after DNA is added with fingers, then lightly tap cuvettes a couple of times on the bench to mix. Put it back On ice before proceeding to the next samples.
Chill all sample On ice for 00:30:00 .
While cells are chilling On ice : Set up the machine: select for Manual at 2.0 ms- this will automatically set for 6 kV.
Wipe cuvette dry with kimwipe to remove condensation and ice.
Start with the "H 2 O control". Electroporate each sample; make sure it makes connection to the metal. If a sample "arc", it is no longer good.
Put each On ice before recovery them.
Take the tubs with SOC from 37 °C .
Transfer each of the cell + DNA (~26-28u) to the labeled tubes. Put each cuvettes back On ice .
Wash each curettes with 100 µL of warm SOC and transfer to recovery tubes.
Put the recovery culture at 37 °C for 01:00:00 with shaking.

Prepare 4 tubes (D0- D3) for each sample for a serial dilution with LB as below. Mix well. Sample reactions can be combined to represent the larger plate plating with 2 mL - 3 mL of recovery culture. Then modify D0 dilution accordingly and dilution factors. A B C D E F 1 Starting (S) D0 D1 D2 D3 2 Volume 1ml 10ul of S + 990 ul of LB 100ul of D0 + 900ul of LB 100ul of D0 + 900ul of LB 100ul of D0 + 900ul of LB 3 Plating No plating 100ul 100ul 100ul 4 Dilution 1:100 1:10,000 1:100,000 1:1,000,000
For each sample and Puc 19, label three 10 cm LB + amp plates (D1-D3) and plate 100 µL from the D1-D3 corresponding tubes. For negative control and H 2 O, plate 100 µL from the 1 mL recovery culture.
Spread the plated culture with beads.
Place the plates in 37 °C for overnight/faster growth.

Pipette 2 mL - 3 mL of recovery culture to each large LB+Carb plate, use the roller to spread the cell cultures. Leave the lids ajar slightly to dry the surface. No need to plate controls on the large plates.
Put all plates in 30 °C for 16-20 hour incubation
Chill one tube of 50 mL LB/plate in 4 °C for harvesting the next day.
The next day, count the colonies on D2 and D3 dilution plates to estimate total colonies on each large plate. For 2 x 10 8 barcodes, there should be 200 colonies on the D3 plates.
The large plates must be harvested and prepped with a preferred plasmid Maxi prep kit immediately.
creator: Melinda K. Wheelock
